crops.name: Maize
tips: Use high-quality hybrid seeds for better yields.
Ensure field is well-weeded during the first 6 weeks.
Apply organic mulch to retain moisture.

crops.name: Common Beans
tips: Rotate with maize or sorghum to break pest cycles.
Do not weed when leaves are wet to prevent disease spread.
Harvest when pods are dry and brittle.

crops.name: Cassava
tips: Plant on mounds or ridges to prevent waterlogging.
Remove and burn plants showing leaf mosaic symptoms immediately.

diseases.name: Maize Streak Virus
diseases.diagnosis: Yellow or white stripes running parallel to leaf veins.
diseases.treatment: Plant resistant varieties; control leafhoppers early; remove infected plants.
diseases.prevention: Early planting with the first rains.

diseases.name: Fall Armyworm
diseases.diagnosis: Circular to irregular holes in leaves; presence of green/brown caterpillars with an inverted Y on head.
diseases.treatment: Handpick larvae for small plots; use Neem extract or recommended pesticides; apply sand/ash in the whorl.
diseases.prevention: Intercropping maize with beans or desmodium.

diseases.name: Cassava Mosaic Disease
diseases.diagnosis: Leaves are small, twisted, and have yellow-green patches.
diseases.treatment: There is no cure once infected; uproot and burn infected plants.

diseases.name: Nutrient Deficiency (Nitrogen/Phosphorus)
diseases.diagnosis: Yellowing starting from leaf tips (Nitrogen) or purplish tints on leaves (Phosphorus).
diseases.treatment: Apply compost, well-rotted manure, or balanced fertilizers.
diseases.prevention: Soil testing and regular addition of organic matter.
